description_long: This house features a symmetrical facade with a prominent central entryway, flanked by two-story brick sections and large vertical windows. The main entrance is elevated with a set of steps, a small porch, and decorative columns, giving it a stately appearance. The roof is primarily flat with a distinctive curved section at the rear, which covers a large windowed area. The side elevations show a mix of window sizes and arrangements, with some asymmetry in the window placement. The rear of the house includes a large, curved roof over a wall of windows, suggesting a spacious interior living area. The exterior materials are mainly red brick and white plaster or concrete, with glass used extensively for windows.
description: A stately two-story brick house with a flat and curved roof, large vertical windows, and a prominent central entryway with columns and steps.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with steps, columns, and large vertical windows on either side.
description_view_1: Side view revealing a mix of window sizes, brick walls, and the transition to the curved rear roof.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by a large curved roof section and expansive windows beneath it.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with more vertical windows and a clear view of the flat roof sections.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows on front, asymmetrical on sides, large picture windows at rear
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with steps, small porch, decorative columns, and a prominent overhang
